proofs.proof_tex: Proof. Fix q in (0,1/2], a measurable plug-in nuisance triple bar_eta=(bar_mu_0,bar_mu_1,bar_e), and write bar_e_q(x)=min{1-q,max{q,bar_e(x)}}. Let Delta_a=bar_mu_a-mu_a and b_q(x)=E_P[Gamma_q(O;bar_eta)|X=x]-tau_P(x). By lem:clip-bias, b_q(x)=(bar_e_q(x)-e_P(x)) (Delta_1(x)/bar_e_q(x)+Delta_0(x)/(1-bar_e_q(x))). Since q <= bar_e_q <= 1-q, both denominators are at least q. Let D_pi={x: pi(x) != pi_star(x)}. The multiplier pi-pi_star is zero outside D_pi and has absolute value at most one, so Cauchy-Schwarz gives |E_P[(pi(X)-pi_star(X)) b_q(X)]| <= P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} ||b_q||_{L2(P_X)}. The displayed identity and the denominator lower bound give ||b_q||_{L2(P_X)} <= q^{-1} ||bar_e_q-e_P||_{L2(P_X)} (||Delta_1||_{L2(P_X)}+||Delta_0||_{L2(P_X)}). Under ass:nuisance-rate, the two regression-error norms are bounded by r_mu_n, up to an absolute factor, hence |E_P[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= C q^{-1} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} r_mu_n ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2. Finally, by the triangle inequality, ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar_e-e_P||_2+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2 <= r_e_n+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2. This proves the asserted reduction. Notice that no step identifies the support of bar_e_q-bar_e with the true weak-overlap set {p_P<=q}; that is precisely the remaining uncontrolled term.

added_lemmas.statement: For q in (0,1/2], any measurable plug-in nuisance triple bar_eta=(bar_mu_0,bar_mu_1,bar_e), and b_q(x)=E_P[Gamma_q(O;bar_eta)|X=x]-tau_P(x), every deterministic pi satisfies |E_P[(pi(X)-pi_star(X)) b_q(X)]| <= q^{-1} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 (||Delta_1||_2+||Delta_0||_2). Under ass:nuisance-rate this is at most C q^{-1} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} r_mu_n (r_e_n+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Proof. Fix q in (0,1/2], a measurable plug-in nuisance triple bar_eta=(bar_mu_0,bar_mu_1,bar_e), and write bar_e_q(x)=min{1-q,max{q,bar_e(x)}}. Let Delta_a=bar_mu_a-mu_a and b_q(x)=E_P[Gamma_q(O;bar_eta)|X=x]-tau_P(x). By lem:clip-bias, b_q(x)=(bar_e_q(x)-e_P(x)) (Delta_1(x)/bar_e_q(x)+Delta_0(x)/(1-bar_e_q(x))). Since q <= bar_e_q <= 1-q, both denominators are at least q. Let D_pi={x: pi(x) != pi_star(x)}. The multiplier pi-pi_star is zero outside D_pi and has absolute value at most one, so Cauchy-Schwarz gives |E_P[(pi(X)-pi_star(X)) b_q(X)]| <= P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} ||b_q||_{L2(P_X)}. The displayed identity and the denominator lower bound give ||b_q||_{L2(P_X)} <= q^{-1} ||bar_e_q-e_P||_{L2(P_X)} (||Delta_1||_{L2(P_X)}+||Delta_0||_{L2(P_X)}). Under ass:nuisance-rate, the two regression-error norms are bounded by r_mu_n, up to an absolute factor, hence |E_P[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= C q^{-1} P_X(D_pi)^{1/2} r_mu_n ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2. Finally, by the triangle inequality, ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar_e-e_P||_2+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2 <= r_e_n+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2. This proves the asserted reduction. proposed_statement_changes.current: CONSTRUCT-AND-DETERMINE. Assume gamma<=alpha+1, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:vc-localized-envelope. For the plain cross-fit clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm with clip q chosen by the analysis, DERIVE (do NOT pre-specify) the best worst-case welfare-regret exponent r_feas with sup_{P in P_class(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas} (log n)^p, by balancing the localized variance against the estimated-nuisance CLIP BIAS over q. State the clip q and the resulting r_feas in closed form, confirm r_feas<=r_star, and reduce at gamma=0.
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: OPEN: determine the welfare-regret rate attainable by the genuinely feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under weak overlap. The present frozen core proves the sharp full-class converse M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} and reduces the feasible upper route to a localized empirical-process term plus the exact product-form clipped-score drift of lem:clip-bias and lem:feasible-drift-product-bound. It does not prove a closed-form feasible exponent r_feas or a balancing clip rule over the full class P_class(alpha,gamma).
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The advertised balancing step is not derivable from the frozen dependencies. Lem:clip-bias and lem:feasible-drift-product-bound reduce the feasible drift to q^{-1} r_mu_n ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2, with ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 <= r_e_n+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2. The remaining displacement ||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2 is supported on the estimated clipping event {bar_e<q} union {bar_e>1-q}, while ass:marginal-overlap controls only the true event {p_P<=q}. A global L2 rate for bar_e-e_P gives no event-inclusion or sup-norm control localizing estimated clipping to true weak overlap. Thus the route's bound B(q) <= r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} is not proved. At gamma=0 the stated marginal-overlap convention gives no vanishing q-tail, so the strict-overlap reduction also does not follow from the current assumptions.

proposed_statement_changes.current: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (sharp converse, thm:minimax-lower, all gamma) and r_feas the DERIVED plain-clipped-AIPW exponent (oeq:feasible-upper), r_feas<=r_star with equality at gamma=0. FIRST welfare-regret rate under weak (one-sided polynomial) overlap, extending strict-overlap policy learning. The r_star-r_feas gap (positive for gamma>0) is the weak-overlap nuisance-estimation cost; tightness is open (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: Under ass:margin-window and assuming Pi contains the two witness-optimal policies used in thm:minimax-lower, the minimax welfare regret satisfies the proved converse bound M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, where r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). Determining any matching or loose feasible upper bound for the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under weak overlap is open on the present frozen core; in particular no closed-form feasible exponent r_feas is proved here.
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The current two-sided bracket depends on oeq:feasible-upper. Since that feasible upper theorem remains open, the displayed upper bound, the claimed closed-form r_feas, the comparison r_feas<=r_star, and the gamma=0 equality clause are not established. The surviving proved content is exactly the sharp converse supplied by thm:minimax-lower, together with the isolated feasible-drift obstruction recorded in the open obligation.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Derive a valid closed-form welfare-regret upper bound for the genuinely feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under the frozen assumptions, or prove that no such bound of the advertised clipping-balance form can hold on the present class.
open_obligations.obstruction: The exact drift identity gives b_q(x)=(bar_e_q(x)-e_P(x))(Delta_1(x)/bar_e_q(x)+Delta_0(x)/(1-bar_e_q(x))). The proved product bound leaves q^{-1} r_mu_n ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2. Splitting ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 gives r_e_n plus ||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2. The second term is controlled by the estimated clipping event, not the true weak-overlap event. The frozen assumptions give no sup-norm, relative-error, or event-localization condition implying {bar_e<q} subset {p_P<=c q} up to negligible mass. Therefore ass:marginal-overlap cannot be applied to the estimated clipping displacement. The gamma=0 endpoint is also not closed because the marginal-overlap convention v^(1/gamma)=1 gives no vanishing tail as q decreases.
open_obligations.attempted: Route 1: apply Cauchy-Schwarz directly to lem:clip-bias. This proves lem:feasible-drift-product-bound but leaves ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 nonlocal. Route 2: split ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 into ||bar_e-e_P||_2 plus ||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2 and try to bound the latter by q P{p_P<=q}^{1/2}. This fails because the support is determined by bar_e, not e_P. Route 3: combine thm:margin-localization and ass:vc-localized-envelope. This recovers the oracle stochastic scale n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), but it does not control the feasible drift. Route 4: use the crude global bound ||bar_e_q-e_P||_2 <= r_e_n+1. This yields no frontier-advancing polynomial regret rate after optimizing q.
open_obligations.partial_result: The sharp converse benchmark M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} is already proved by thm:minimax-lower. The feasible achievability route is reduced to two explicit pieces: an oracle localized empirical-process term of order n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), and the product-form clipped-score drift bounded by C q^{-1} r_mu_n (r_e_n+||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2) P_X(D_pi)^{1/2}. The only missing mathematical step is a valid localization or counterexample for ||bar_e_q-bar_e||_2 under the current nuisance and overlap assumptions.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Determine whether any genuinely feasible estimator, and in particular the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, can attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) under weak overlap, or whether feasible weak-arm nuisance learning necessarily forces a strictly slower welfare-regret rate.
open_obligations.obstruction: This tightness question cannot be settled until oeq:feasible-upper is closed or replaced by a feasible-estimator lower obstruction. The present frozen core proves the lower benchmark r_star through thm:minimax-lower, but it proves neither a feasible upper rate nor a feasible-only lower bound showing that all implementable procedures are slower.
open_obligations.attempted: I reduced tightness to the exact feasible-drift obstruction. The oracle empirical-process side is not the barrier; the unresolved term is the estimated clipping displacement in lem:feasible-drift-product-bound. No dependency in the frozen core converts that term into a rate, and no existing target proves an impossibility theorem for feasible estimators.
open_obligations.partial_result: The converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) is fully identified and proved as a minimax lower benchmark on the full class. The residual feasible-tightness problem is now sharply formulated: either devise a feasible estimator/proof that controls the nonlocal product drift at the r_star scale, or prove that this drift creates a strictly slower feasible frontier.
